100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 21, 1926, the British Empire faced mounting tensions as strikes continued to disrupt coal production, igniting fears of a national crisis. In the United States, the Senate was engulfed in debates over the proposed tariff bill, reflecting ongoing economic concerns. Meanwhile, France's political landscape shifted with the resignation of Prime Minister Paul Painlevé, signaling instability in the wake of ongoing economic strife.
Updated: Sunday, June 21, 2026 at 08:27:38 AM CDT
